story.iWant: to enable OpenAI prompt caching for system prompts
story.soThat: I can reduce LLM costs by 30-50% for cached tokens without any quality impact

task.title: Restructure system prompt với static content first

subtasks.subtask: Identify static sections (default system prompt, agent builder prompt, tool schemas)
Identify dynamic sections (knowledge base, datetime, user-specific content)
Reorder prompt sections: static content first, dynamic content last
Ensure total prompt size > 1,024 tokens after restructuring
Test prompt structure với sample requests
Integration test với actual LLM calls
Confirm prompt size > 1,024 tokens (OpenAI caching threshold)
Verify static content is at the beginning of prompt
Test với OpenAI Compatible API (v98store) để confirm caching works
Document caching requirements và best practices
Verify cache hit detection trong responses
Add cache metrics to monitoring dashboard
Create cache hit rate tracking và reporting

acceptanceCriteria.ac: System prompt được restructure với static content first (để enable caching)
Prompts đảm bảo > 1,024 tokens (threshold cho OpenAI caching)
Monitor `cached_tokens` trong responses và log metrics
Cache hit rate được track và report trong monitoring dashboard
No quality degradation (100% similarity với original responses)

doc.title: ChainLens AI Worker - Epic Breakdown: LLM Optimization
doc.snippet: Story 1.1 defines the requirements for enabling OpenAI prompt caching. Expected savings: $18-27/month with zero quality impact. Effort: 30 minutes.

doc.title: ChainLens AI Worker - Master Optimization Plan v1.1
doc.section: Phase 1: Quality-Preserving Quick Wins - Task 1.1: Enable OpenAI Prompt Caching
doc.snippet: OpenAI prompt caching requires prompts ≥ 1,024 tokens. Static content must be placed first. Cached tokens receive 90% discount. No code changes needed - chỉ cần restructure prompt order.

doc.title: Research Prompt: System Prompt Optimization
doc.snippet: OpenAI and compatible APIs provide automatic prompt caching for prompts ≥ 1,024 tokens. Cache increments: 128-token chunks. TTL: 5-10 minutes. Cost reduction: Up to 50% for cached tokens. Latency reduction: Up to 80%.

doc.title: Code Review: LLM Chat Processing & System Prompt Optimization
doc.snippet: Current system prompt structure in `backend/core/run.py::PromptManager.build_system_prompt()`. Issues identified: System prompt bloat, redundant information, tool schema duplication.

file.reason: Primary method to modify for prompt restructuring. Currently builds prompt by concatenating sections in order: default system prompt, agent-specific, builder prompt, knowledge base, MCP tools, tool schemas, datetime. Need to reorder: static sections first, dynamic sections last.
LLM API call handler. Need to extract `cached_tokens` from response usage metrics. Response includes usage information with cached_tokens field for OpenAI Compatible APIs.

Reference implementation for Anthropic caching. Shows patterns for cache control directives, token counting, and cache management. Can reference for monitoring patterns but focus on OpenAI Compatible API.
Returns static builder prompt content (~300 tokens). This is a static section that should be placed early in the prompt for caching.
Returns default system prompt (~1,000 tokens). This is a static section that should be placed first in the prompt for caching.

constraint.description: Zero quality impact required. Cached responses must maintain 100% similarity with original responses.
Dual-mode architecture: Original mode (baseline) + Optimized mode (with caching). Feature flags allow switching and rollback.
Must work with OpenAI Compatible API (v98store). Current models: gpt-4o-mini, qwen3-30b, deepseek-v3-1, qwen3-235b, claude-haiku-4-5.
Prompts must exceed 1,024 tokens to trigger OpenAI caching. Current prompt size: ~4,350 tokens (meets requirement).
Minimal code changes. Only restructure prompt order in `PromptManager.build_system_prompt()`. No new files required.
Must not break existing LLM calls. Changes should be transparent to callers. Test with existing agent configurations.

interface.name: PromptManager.build_system_prompt()
interface.signature: async def build_system_prompt(model_name: str, agent_config: Optional[dict], thread_id: str, mcp_wrapper_instance: Optional[MCPToolWrapper], client=None, tool_registry=None, xml_tool_calling: bool = True) -> dict

interface.name: make_llm_api_call()
interface.signature: async def make_llm_api_call(messages: List[Dict[str, Any]], model_name: str, response_format: Optional[Any] = None, temperature: float = 0, max_tokens: Optional[int] = None, tools: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None, tool_choice: str = "auto", api_key: Optional[str] = None, api_base: Optional[str] = None, stream: bool = True, top_p: Optional[float] = None, model_id: Optional[str] = None, headers: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None, extra_headers: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None) -> Union[Dict[str, Any], AsyncGenerator, ModelResponse]

tests.standards: Testing standards include: Quality validation (100% similarity check), Performance testing (cache hit rate, cost savings, latency), Integration testing (existing LLM calls, different models, agent configurations). Use semantic similarity checking for quality validation. A/B testing framework for comparing original vs optimized modes.

test.idea: Unit test: Verify prompt restructuring logic correctly identifies static vs dynamic sections and reorders them
Integration test: Test prompt structure with actual LLM calls to verify caching works
Test: Verify prompt size > 1,024 tokens after restructuring
Test: Verify static content is at the beginning of prompt (first 1,024+ tokens)
Unit test: Extract cached_tokens from LLM response usage object
Integration test: Verify cache metrics are logged and appear in monitoring dashboard
Automated test: Compare cached vs non-cached responses using semantic similarity (target: 100% similarity)
A/B test: Run same queries with original and optimized modes, compare quality metrics
